title: 3 Great Value AI Stocks (Hint: 1 is a Household Name; the Other 2 You've Never Heard Of)
author: Lee Samaha
description: The article identifies three AI stocks offering good value: Alphabet (Google), which is investing heavily in AI infrastructure with potential for strong future free cash flow; Brady Corporation, benefiting from rapid data center-related wire identification product growth and the upcoming Honeywell PSS acquisition; and Belden, positioned to benefit from both data center buildout through 2030 and subsequent inference spending growth, with the RUCKUS Networks acquisition expanding its enterprise networking exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong AI infrastructure investment positioning with projected steady 30% FCF margins and $1 trillion+ revenue by 2030s, potentially reaching $6.7 trillion valuation. Capital spending expected to moderate as data center buildout completes.

Strong AI infrastructure investment positioning with projected steady 30% FCF margins and $1 trillion+ revenue by 2030s, potentially reaching $6.7 trillion valuation. Capital spending expected to moderate as data center buildout completes.

Data center wire identification products growing 19% and 13% across regions, significantly outpacing overall company growth. Honeywell PSS acquisition is transformative, combining mobile scanning expertise with labeling capabilities. Trading at attractive 14.4x 2027 earnings.

Well-positioned for both data center buildout through 2030 and inference spending growth thereafter. RUCKUS Networks acquisition increases enterprise networking exposure. Trading at significant discount to peers like TE Connectivity and Amphenol despite double-digit data center exposure growth.
Mentioned as a peer comparison point; trades at premium valuation relative to Belden despite similar data center exposure, suggesting less attractive value proposition.
Mentioned as a peer comparison point; trades at premium valuation relative to Belden despite similar data center exposure, suggesting less attractive value proposition.

title: Nasdaq 100 Hits Record Above 30,000 On Micron's 18% Rally: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: The Nasdaq 100 crossed 30,000 during intraday trading as semiconductor stocks surged, led by Micron Technology's 18% gain following a UBS analyst price target hike to $1,625. The rally was driven by increased AI spending expectations from hyperscalers, with chip stocks dominating market leadership. The S&P 500 rose 0.6%, while the Russell 2000 outperformed with a 1.7% gain. AutoZone plunged 10.1% after missing revenue estimates despite beating EPS.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 18% on UBS analyst price target hike to $1,625 (from $535), driven by strong AI memory demand expectations. Market cap exceeded $1 trillion for the first time, with stock up 200% year-to-date.
Jumped 12.3% tracking the broader semiconductor rally and benefiting from announced cash dividend, with no company-specific negative catalyst.
Rose 9.1% on sympathy buying with the semiconductor sector rotation, driven by positive AI infrastructure spending sentiment.
Climbed 8.7% lifted by the chip sector rotation and increased AI-related spending expectations.
Added 8.4% as part of the broader semiconductor and tech sector rally driven by AI infrastructure demand.
Gained 6.8% as cheaper jet fuel and Iran deal narrative boosted airline stocks, lifting the broader airline sector.
Plunged 10.1% despite beating EPS estimates, due to revenue miss ($4.84B vs $4.88B estimate) and soft domestic same-store sales of 4.1% against elevated expectations.
Dropped 6% as investors trimmed positions ahead of May 28 earnings announcement.

title: Earnings Volatility Watch: What To Expect From Sandisk and 5 Mag Seven Stocks This Week
author: Piero Cingari
description: Ten mega-cap companies representing $18.59 trillion in combined market capitalization report earnings this week. SanDisk leads with the largest implied volatility at 16.57%, up 317% YTD on AI-driven NAND demand. Five Magnificent Seven stocks report with modest 2.7% average implied moves, reflecting gains already priced in. Storage and semiconductor names dominate the top volatility list, while Qualcomm faces negative growth estimates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Up 317% YTD with 55.81% gain in April alone; highest implied move (16.57%) signals strong market expectations; AI-driven NAND cycle tightening pricing and lifting operating leverage
Third best-performing S&P 500 stock in 2026 with 134.52% YTD gain; 49.36% of gains in April; strong NAND cycle beneficiary
Seventh best-performing S&P 500 stock with 112.88% YTD gain; 49.65% of returns in April; strong earnings growth expected (+84.23% EPS)
Eighth best-performing S&P 500 stock, up 100.88% YTD with 29.36% April gain; optical fiber and AI data-center glass driving rally; strong earnings growth projected (+28.07% EPS)
High implied volatility (11.70%) but facing negative growth estimates with EPS down 10.31% and revenue off 2.36%; process-control tools at leading edge but near-term headwinds
Only mega-cap on top 10 facing negative growth estimates; down 12.42% YTD on smartphone-cycle concerns and Apple modem-share losses; 12.01% implied move reflects uncertainty
Modest 1.16% implied move suggests limited volatility expected; up only 6.3% month-to-date compared to other Mag 7 peers; strong rally may already be priced in

title: DesignCon 2026 Wraps with Focus on Innovation, Artificial Intelligence
author: Informa Markets Manufacturing
description: DesignCon 2026, the premier conference for chip, board, and systems design, concluded its 31st edition with record-breaking attendance at Santa Clara Convention Center. The event featured keynote speakers from Purdue University, Agentrys, and NASA, covering topics including AI infrastructure, quantum internet technologies, and 5G advancements. The global high-speed communications sector is projected to reach $975 billion in annual sales in 2026, driven by AI infrastructure growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company showcased latest products and solutions at a record-breaking industry conference, indicating strong market presence and innovation in high-speed communications sector.
Featured as a leading company exhibiting at the premier industry event, demonstrating active participation in advancing chip and systems design technologies.
Highlighted as a leading company showcasing solutions at DesignCon, benefiting from the growing $975 billion high-speed communications market driven by AI infrastructure boom.
Provided live demonstrations and interactive exhibits of next-generation technologies at the record-attendance conference, indicating strong engagement in the expanding AI and semiconductor markets.

title: SATA HDD Connector Market Trends and Opportunities Worldwide, 2026-2032
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global SATA HDD connector market is projected to grow from USD 1.03 billion in 2026 to USD 1.47 billion by 2032, with a CAGR of 5.93%. Growth is driven by sustained adoption of standardized storage solutions across enterprise, surveillance, industrial, and consumer platforms, despite emerging new protocols. Key trends include increased focus on reliability, compliance, automation-ready designs, and dual-source supply strategies to mitigate regional disruptions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key company in the growing SATA HDD connector market with strong demand across enterprise, surveillance, and industrial sectors. Market growth of 5.93% CAGR through 2032 presents expansion opportunities.
Profiled as a major player in the SATA HDD connector market. Increasing demand for reliable connectors in mission-critical systems and automation-ready designs supports positive outlook.

Included as a key company in the SATA HDD connector ecosystem. Sustained adoption of SATA interfaces in enterprise and consumer storage platforms supports continued relevance and market opportunities.
